edges.description: Reduced KANSL1 dosage perturbs neuronal homeostatic programs that culminate in autophagosome accumulation.
Autophagosome accumulation at excitatory synapses precedes impaired synaptic transmission and neuronal network activity.

nodes.description: In KANSL1-deficient human neurons, reduced SOD1 is associated with increased oxidative stress and subsequent autophagosome accumulation, including at excitatory synapses.

evidence.reference_title: Imbalanced autophagy causes synaptic deficits in a human model for neurodevelopmental disorders.
evidence.snippet: we identified SOD1 (superoxide dismutase 1), an antioxidant enzyme, to be significantly decreased, leading to a subsequent increase in oxidative stress and autophagosome accumulation.

evidence.reference_title: The Koolen-de Vries syndrome: a phenotypic comparison of patients with a 17q21.31 microdeletion versus a KANSL1 sequence variant.
evidence.snippet: Comparison of the phenotype of both the microdeletion and SNV patients does not show differences of clinical importance, stressing that haploinsufficiency of KANSL1 is sufficient to cause the full KdVS phenotype.
KANSL1 | HGNC:24565 | Koolen-de Vries syndrome | MONDO:0012496 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Heterozygous loss of KANSL1 is the core molecular lesion in KdVS. KANSL1 is a chromatin-associated regulator within the NSL complex, linking reduced dosage to altered chromatin organization and transcriptional control during brain development.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in KANSL1 cause the 17q21.31 microdeletion syndrome phenotype.
evidence.snippet: These findings indicate that 17q21.31 deletion syndrome is a monogenic disorder caused by haploinsufficiency of KANSL1.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the chromatin modifier gene KANSL1 cause the 17q21.31 microdeletion syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The KANSL1 protein is an evolutionarily conserved regulator of the chromatin modifier KAT8, which influences gene expression through histone H4 lysine 16 (H4K16) acetylation.

nodes.description: Autophagosome accumulation at excitatory synapses in KANSL1-deficient neurons is associated with reduced synaptic density, decreased AMPA receptor-mediated transmission, and impaired neuronal network activity.

evidence.reference_title: Imbalanced autophagy causes synaptic deficits in a human model for neurodevelopmental disorders.
evidence.snippet: In KANSL1-deficient neurons, autophagosome accumulation at excitatory synapses resulted in reduced synaptic density, reduced GRIA/AMPA receptor-mediated transmission and impaired neuronal network activity.
